title: iris zagrica subsp. zagrica
intro: Iris zagrica subsp. zagrica, also known as the Balkan iris, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Iridaceae. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine, particularly in the Balkan region, for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant is believed to provide health benefits such as reducing fever, soothing sore throats, and alleviating respiratory issues. Iris zagrica subsp. zagrica is often prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, allowing for its medicinal properties to be extracted and utilized in a variety of ways.
